#domain/tooling #domain/codegen

## XOR-rendered doc sections hide newly added content

A code generator that renders section A *or* section B (never both) creates a
silent visibility trap: content added to the dormant branch never appears.

- The branch is **mutually exclusive (XOR)**, not additive — one early-return
  short-circuits the other.
- Adding to the suppressed branch produces no error and no output; the omission
  is invisible until the dominant branch is emptied.
- Diagnose by asking "what condition gates this section?" before "why is my new
  content missing?" — the gate, not the content, is the bug.

The general rule: when a render path has an early return that picks one of two
surfaces, anything you add to the loser is dead until the winner is removed.

**From this session:** `render-readme.mjs` early-returns the `## Commands`
section whenever a plugin has ANY command, so the new skills only rendered after
the v1 commands were deleted — Commands and Skills are XOR, not additive.

Related: [[Early return as control flow]], [[Generated docs drift from source]]
Sources: https://dev.to/noureldin2303/conditional-rendering-best-practices-and-pitfalls-to-avoid-3eae
